title: Lastrea hexagonoptera
intro: Lastrea hexagonoptera, also known as the hexagon fern, is a species of fern native to tropical Africa, characterized by its distinctive hexagonal leaflets. The hexagon fern has been traditionally used in folk medicine, offering various health benefits, including the treatment of fever, rheumatism, and respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and coughs. The bioactive constituents of Lastrea hexagonoptera include flavonoids, saponins, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Traditionally, the hexagon fern is prepared as a decoction or infusion, and is also used in the form of a poultice or ointment to treat various ailments, although its use should be under the guidance of a healthcare professional.
taxonomy: Lastrea hexagonoptera is a species of fern in the family Dryopteridaceae. It is native to the Himalayan region and parts of Asia. This plant belongs to the genus Lastrea, which is a group of ferns that are often epiphytic or terrestrial. The classification of Lastrea hexagonoptera is as follows: Kingdom: Plantae, Division: Pteridophyta, Class: Polypodiopsida, Order: Polypodiales, Family: Dryopteridaceae, Genus: Lastrea, Species: L. hexagonoptera. Further research is needed to confirm its classification with certainty.
